title: Proteomic investigation of the effect of PDE4 inhibitor Crisaborole on atopic dermatitis

findings.executive_summary: DIA mass spectrometry proteomics from a clinical atopic dermatitis study comparing the PDE4 inhibitor Crisaborole (n=8) against the corticosteroid Betamethasone (n=7), using tape-strip biopsies sampled at baseline and post-treatment. After Spectronaut-aware normalisation and KNN imputation, differential abundance was modelled with limma using paired and linear mixed-model designs on treated AD lesions, with normal skin and untreated AD lesions serving as stability controls to isolate treatment-driven signal. Pathway enrichment via fgsea (KEGG, Reactome, GO) characterised the mechanisms modulated by each drug, with findings cross-checked against the known biology of Crisaborole (keratinocyte-acting) and Betamethasone (inflammatory-infiltrate-acting). Analysis prioritised robustness throughout, given the small sample size, recruitment having been reduced from 40 patients due to COVID-19.
next_steps: Finalise sensitivity comparison between paired t-test and mixed-model results
Complete literature-based mechanistic validation of treatment-driven signals

embedding.source_text: Proteomic investigation of the effect of PDE4 inhibitor Crisaborole on atopic dermatitis | Clinical DIA proteomics comparing Crisaborole (PDE4 inhibitor) vs Betamethasone (corticosteroid) on treated AD lesions, with normal skin and untreated AD as stability controls; limma differential abundance with paired and mixed-model designs, fgsea pathway enrichment. | Methods: differential abundance, pathway enrichment, dimensionality reduction, linear mixed modelling | Data: proteomics, mass spectrometry | Disease: atopic dermatitis | Keywords: crisaborole, betamethasone, PDE4 inhibitor, treatment response, tape strip biopsy | Tools: limma, proDA, fgsea, msigdbr
